claim: On September 11, 2001, CBS News and CNN reported that NYPD had stopped a van loaded with explosives on the George Washington Bridge. NYPD Commissioner Bernard Kerik publicly contradicted these accounts at a press conference the same day, stating no explosives had been recovered. The initial reports were retracted; the FBI has stated it has no records pertaining to this specific incident.

sources.label: Wikipedia — “Timeline for September following the September 11 attacks”
sources.quote: van filled with explosives has been stopped on the George Washington Bridge

sources.label: Bernard Kerik press conference, 9/11/2001 — answering a question about explosives in a van on the George Washington Bridge (video extract)
Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: What is documented: CBS and CNN ran the explosives-van story on 9/11 itself; Kerik denied it the same day; no van/explosives confirmed in any subsequent official record. What the reel does: treats the initial reports as the truth and the retraction as the cover-up. Both layers are real; which one to believe is the editorial question this record presents. [meta tags removed from themes array, kept here for record state: contested]
